audit_items.proof_trace: The file has exactly one allocation site (readString line 85: zmalloc) and two free sites (process line 125: normal-path zfree; process line 130: cleanup-block zfree) that are mutually exclusive per allocation — a for-loop body completes without break (line 125 reached) XOR the loop exits early via break (i < argc, line 130 reached). readString always sets *target = NULL before any allocation attempt, so callers always receive a defined value. After each zfree at line 125, str is immediately overwritten by the next readString call (which resets *target = NULL as its first statement), and after any while-loop exit, str is never referenced again (lines 135–141 use only pos/error/multi/feof).
